---
title: Life Hacking for Educators
subtitle: JSM 2026
listing:
  id: talks
  contents: talks/*/index.qmd
  fields: [image, title, author, subtitle]
  type: grid
  sort:
    - filename
---

![](https://ww2.amstat.org/meetings/jsm/2026/images/logo.png){style="float: right;" fig-alt="JSM 2026 Logo" width="150"}

Thursday, Aug 6\
8:30 AM - 10:20 AM\
[Session \# 1524](https://ww3.aievolution.com/JSMAnnual2026/Events/viewEv?ev=6547)\
Oregon Convention Center Room: CC-107A

## Talks

::: {#talks}
:::

## Overview

Life hacks help us streamline our lives and make us feel clever---a not insignificant source of joy in these difficult times. 
As educators, many of us are invigorated by the positive energy we get from our students, and enervated by the drudgery of administrative tasks, often made more manual and more painful by opaque software systems. 
In this session, we celebrate our most clever and useful tricks for minimizing the headaches we incur from learning management systems and other academic edifices (e.g., Moodle, Canvas, Workday, etc.)

**Session chair:** [Andrew Bray](https://bids.berkeley.edu/people/andrew-bray) - Berkeley

**Session organizer:** [Ben Baumer](https://beanumber.github.io/) - Smith College

## Homepage

All session materials can be viewed at <https://beanumber.github.io/quarto-life-hacks-jsm26/>.
The GitHub repo that serves this site is at <https://github.com/beanumber/quarto-life-hacks-jsm26/>.
